summary: The patient has a sudden-onset ataxic gait and altered responsiveness consistent with an acute upper motor neuron lesion. Given her history of Down syndrome—which is associated with increased risk of spinal cord abnormalities such as syringomyelia—the diagnosis should consider underlying structural causes due to congenital vulnerabilities linked to this condition.
